race.comment: While TORI'S DEE (8) doesn't have much of a second-up record - 5 starts for 1 minor placing - his effort to get within 2-1/4 lengths of the talented I'mateez in a BM62 first-up was probably the best last-start performance of anything in the race. He'll be fitter for that and, as he showed that day, he can race on-speed which is often an advantage here. STEWART (3) has been racing consistently in this grade with two wins from four starts, so you can't knock the way he's going. His big problem is trying to overcome the wide gate. BRAZEN FLING (9) was plain last start, but that was in 62 grade and the start prior went close in a 56 grade of race that looked a touch stronger than what he meets here. MR MARKLE (2) has been beaten less than a length his past two, most recently when he flashed home late, and strikes the right race to record win No 2.
